channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Homing
item.description:
Source: TW

Heard of a weird case of an old dog from a German guy: The original owners had given their dog away to a friend. They then moved far from their home to a different part of the city beyond a certain river. Their former dog suddenly appeared years later at their new place. It stayed with them for a night and then returned to its current place. It died a few days later. The new owner confirmed that it was missing for a day before that.

item.title: Wolf packs
item.description:
Source: TW

Wolf packs

Wolf packs are family units where the dominant reproductive pair, the patriarch and matriarch, are supported by the rest of their pack, mostly made up of their offspring and sometimes more distant relatives (or more rarely, unrelated adoptees). Wolf fathers commonly:

  • Guard pups.
  • Bring them food.
  • Regurgitate food for pups that have stopped drinking milk.
  • Participate actively in their social development.

Dhole

A similar situation is seen in the dhole, where both parents and other pack members actively participate in rearing the pups.
